title: Sudden Head Tilt in Senior Dogs: Vestibular Disease & Triage Guide
meta_desc: Sudden head tilt in senior dogs is often Vestibular Disease. Learn why this happens, the urgency level, and how to help your vet diagnose it.
content_html:
What is Sudden Head Tilt in Senior Dogs?

When an older dog suddenly develops a head tilt, it is most commonly a condition known as Old Dog Vestibular Syndrome, or Idiopathic Vestibular Disease. The vestibular system is responsible for maintaining balance, and when it is disrupted, dogs may tilt their heads, stumble, or experience involuntary eye flickering known as nystagmus. While it looks very alarming and is often mistaken for a stroke, it is frequently a localized issue in the inner ear or balance centers.

Is it an Emergency?

The urgency level for a sudden head tilt is generally considered Medium. While usually not a life-threatening emergency, your dog should be seen by a veterinarian within 24 hours. A professional evaluation is necessary to rule out more serious causes such as inner ear infections, brain tumors, or inflammatory diseases. If your dog is also experiencing extreme lethargy, collapsing, or cannot breathe properly, you should seek immediate emergency care.

Why a Photo or Video Helps Triage

Taking a clear photo of your dog’s head position and a short video of their movement can be incredibly helpful for your veterinarian. Visual documentation allows the vet to see the severity of the tilt and the specific direction of any eye flickering (nystagmus) before the dog is stressed by a car ride or clinic visit. This helps the clinical team differentiate between a peripheral issue (the inner ear) and a central issue (the brain) more effectively.

faqs.question: Is sudden head tilt the same as a stroke?
faqs.answer: While strokes can cause head tilts, they are relatively rare in dogs. Most cases in senior dogs are diagnosed as Old Dog Vestibular Syndrome, which has a much better prognosis.

faqs.question: How long does it take for a dog to recover from vestibular disease?
faqs.answer: Most dogs show significant improvement within 72 hours, though a slight head tilt may persist for several weeks or even permanently.

faqs.question: Should I withhold food if my dog is tilting their head?
faqs.answer: Since vestibular disease often causes motion sickness and nausea, it is best to offer small amounts of bland food or consult your vet before a full feeding to prevent vomiting.
